lessons.title: What Is Java?
lessons.summary: Understand the features and use cases of the Java programming language

contents.title: What You'll Learn
contents.body:
In this lesson, you'll learn:

- The three main features of Java
- Why Java is widely used in enterprises
- The relationship between Java SE 11/17 versions and certification exams

Let's start by getting a big-picture understanding.

contents.title: Three Key Features of Java
contents.body:
Java has three main features.

| Feature | Details |
|---|---|
| Platform Independent | The same program runs on different operating systems such as Windows, Mac, and Linux |
| Object-Oriented | A way of representing real-world "things" in programs |
| High Safety | Automatic memory management (GC) and strict type checking |

"**Write Once, Run Anywhere**" is the core philosophy of Java.
contents.note: Java SE 11 and SE 17 are the current major certification exam target versions. The learning content in this app is also based on the SE 11/17 exam scope.

contents.title: New Terms Introduced
contents.body:
- [[Garbage Collection]] (GC): A mechanism that automatically frees memory that is no longer needed. Unlike C, you don't have to manage memory manually.

- **Object-Oriented**: You'll learn about this in detail later. For now, just remember it as "a way of representing real-world things in programs."

- **Compile**: Converting human-readable code (source code) into a format that a computer can execute.
contents.note: These terms will appear many times going forward. You don't need to memorize them perfectly right now.

contents.title: Where Java Is Used
contents.body:
Java is widely used around the world.

| Field | Examples |
|---|---|
| Enterprise Systems | Banking, insurance, logistics, etc. |
| Web Applications | E-commerce sites, social networks, etc. |
| Mobile | Android apps |
| Servers | Large-scale server systems |

Java is especially widely adopted for enterprise system development in Japan.
contents.note: Learning Java is a great asset for your career and job opportunities.

contents.title: Key Points
contents.body:
- Java is a language that is "platform independent," "**object-oriented**," and "highly safe"
- It is widely used in enterprise systems and web applications
- This app's content is based on the Java SE 11/17 exam scope

quizzes.question: Which of the following is a core philosophy of Java?
quizzes.explanation: Java was designed with the philosophy "Write Once, Run Anywhere." The same program runs on different operating systems.

quizzes.question: In which field is Java particularly widely used?
quizzes.explanation: Java has a very high adoption rate in enterprise systems (banking, insurance, logistics, etc.). It is especially the de facto standard for enterprise development in Japan.

quizzes.question: Java has a mechanism that automatically frees memory that is no longer needed. This mechanism is called ____.
quizzes.explanation: Java has a mechanism called Garbage Collection (GC) that automatically frees memory that is no longer needed. Programmers don't have to manage it manually.

lessons.title: Understanding the Development Environment
lessons.summary: Understand the JDK, IDE, and how compilation works

contents.title: What You'll Learn
contents.body:
In this lesson, you'll learn:

- The role of the **JDK** (Java Development Kit)
- Types of IDEs (Integrated Development Environments) and how to choose one
- The flow of "Source Code → **Compile** → Execute"

Let's understand the tools needed to write and run Java programs.

contents.title: What Is the JDK?
contents.body:
The **JDK** (Java Development Kit) is the toolbox for creating Java programs.

| Component | Description |
|---|---|
| javac (Compiler) | Converts source code into bytecode |
| java (Runtime) | Runs compiled programs |
| Other Tools | Debugger, documentation generator, etc. |

Once you install the JDK, you can start developing in Java.
contents.note: **JRE** (Java Runtime Environment) is an environment for running programs only. You need the JDK for development.

contents.title: What Is an IDE?
contents.body:
An IDE (Integrated Development Environment) is a convenient tool that lets you write, run, and debug code all in one application.

Popular IDEs for Java:
| IDE | Description |
|---|---|
| Eclipse | Free. Commonly used in training programs in Japan |
| IntelliJ IDEA | Feature-rich. Community Edition is free |
| VS Code | Java support available through extensions |

You can write Java in a simple text editor, but using an IDE greatly improves your productivity.

contents.title: The Compilation and Execution Flow
contents.body: A Java program follows the flow of "Source Code → **Compile** → Execute."
contents.note: .java files are human-readable source code, and .class files are **bytecode** that the JVM can execute.

contents.title: Watch Out for the Difference Between JDK and JRE
contents.body:
This is a point that beginners often find confusing.

| Name | Full Name | Role |
|---|---|---|
| JDK | Java Development Kit | Development + Execution (for developers) |
| JRE | Java Runtime Environment | Execution only (for end users) |

You need the JDK to write programs. With only the JRE, you cannot **compile**.
contents.note: Since Java 11, the JRE is no longer distributed separately. Installing the JDK includes the runtime environment as well.

contents.title: Key Points
contents.body:
- The JDK is an essential toolbox for Java development
- Using an IDE significantly improves development efficiency
- Java follows the flow of "Source Code → **Compile** → Execute"
- .java is the source file, .class is the **bytecode**

quizzes.question: Which of the following is NOT included in the JDK?
quizzes.explanation: The JDK includes the compiler (javac), the runtime (java), and a debugger, among other tools, but it does not include a web browser.

quizzes.question: Arrange the steps for running a Java program in the correct order.
quizzes.explanation: Java follows the flow of: (1) Write the source code → (2) Compile with javac → (3) Execute with the java command.

quizzes.question: What is the file extension of the file generated after compilation?
quizzes.explanation: When you compile with the javac command, a .class file (bytecode) is generated. This is the format that the JVM (Java Virtual Machine) executes.

lessons.title: Your First Program
lessons.summary: Understand the structure of a Hello World program

contents.title: What You'll Learn
contents.body:
In this lesson, you'll learn:

- The basic structure of a Java program ([[Class]] and [[Method]])
- Your first Java program: "Hello World"
- The meaning and boilerplate of the **main method**

Let's understand your first program and take the first step in Java.

contents.title: Hello World
contents.body: The simplest program that displays text on the screen in Java.

contents.title: The main Method Is "Boilerplate"
contents.body:
It's perfectly fine if you don't understand the following code at first.

public static void main([[String]][] args)

This single line is the boilerplate that indicates "the entry point of the program."

| Keyword | Meaning |
|---|---|
| public | Accessible from anywhere |
| static | Can be called directly from the class |
| void | No return value |
| main | The starting point of the program |
| String[] args | Receives command-line arguments |

For now, just remember that "Java programs start with this form."
contents.note: The detailed meaning of each keyword will be covered in Chapter 5: "Methods" and beyond.

contents.title: Program Structure
contents.body:
1. public class Hello
   Java programs are always written inside a "class." The file name must match the class name (Hello.java).

2. public static void main(String[] args)
   This is the "main method." Every Java program starts execution from here. Remember it as boilerplate.

3. System.out.println("Hello, World!");
   This is the command to display text on the screen. Every statement must end with a semicolon (;).
contents.note: Java is case-sensitive. The 'S' in System is uppercase.

contents.title: Key Points
contents.body:
- Java programs are written inside a class
- Execution starts from the main method
- Statements must end with a semicolon (;)
- The file name must match the class name

quizzes.question: What will be displayed when the following program is executed?
quizzes.explanation: System.out.println() displays the string inside the parentheses exactly as-is on the screen.

quizzes.question: The following code has an error. What is the cause?
quizzes.explanation: In Java, every statement must end with a semicolon (;). The code has a compilation error because there is no semicolon after println("Hello").

choices.text: The main method is written incorrectly

quizzes.question: Complete the method that serves as the entry point of a Java program.
quizzes.explanation: The entry point of a Java program is the main method. public static void main(String[] args) is the entry point of a Java program.
